Article.ArticleTitle: Single aperiodically poled lithium niobate chip for simultaneous electro-optic spectral and Q switchings in a cross-polarized, closely-lying dual-wavelength Nd:YLF laser.

Abstract.AbstractText: We report on a fast switchable light source in a Nd:YLF laser oscillating at two cross-polarized, closely lying emission lines (π-polarized 1047-nm and σ-polarized 1053-nm lines), achieved using an intracavity aperiodically poled lithium niobate (APPLN) electro-optic (EO) photonic chip. The APPLN, whose domain structure is built with a simulated annealing optimization algorithm, functions as a wavelength- and voltage-dependent polarization mode converter to manipulate the polarization-dependent laser gain of the dual emission lines in Nd:YLF. When the APPLN operates at characteristic voltages, VHQ = 0, 572, and 723 V, dual cross-polarized 1047- and 1053-nm lines, single π-polarized 1047-nm line, and single σ-polarized 1053-nm line will be gained (i.e., VHQ is a voltage turning the cavity into a high-Q state for a specific laser line(s)) in the laser, respectively, while the gain of both lines will be suppressed at VLQ = 157 V. Fast EO switching among the three different laser spectra (of different polarization states) can thus be done simply by tuning the APPLN operating voltage. Even interestingly, when modulating the APPLN with a voltage pulse train switching between a VHQ and VLQ, we observed a few tens of ns, high peak-power (∼20 kW) laser pulses from the system for the three output spectra (along with their relatively weak non-phase-matched second harmonic and sum frequency signals generated through the APPLN), again switchable with respect to their characteristic voltages VHQ. This system potentially features multiplexability in time, wavelength, and polarization.
